lines.speaker: You
lines.text: You walk off the Meridian Theatre stage and the sound of the room closes behind you like water.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: Your hands are still shaking. You don't know if that was good. You don't know if it mattered.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: Then — footsteps. Unhurried. Deliberate.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: You left the stage on the wrong foot.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Deliberately.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Most people trying to make an impression overdo the exit. You underdid it.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: That's harder to do well than the obvious choice. Either you knew exactly what you were doing...

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: ...or you got very lucky. I haven't decided which yet.

choices.text: "I was just trying to feel genuine."
"I knew exactly what I was doing."
"I didn't realize anyone was still watching."

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Genuine.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: That word gets people killed in this industry. But it's also why some of them survive it.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Good answer.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Whether it's true or not.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Then you should know —

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: I'm always watching.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: I'm going to give you a piece of advice you didn't ask for.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Take it or don't. I only offer it once.

choices.text: "I tend to figure things out myself."
"What makes you qualified to advise me?"

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Stop performing for the room.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Perform for the camera behind the auditioner's left shoulder. It shifts your eye line by two degrees.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Nobody notices consciously. They just feel it.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Smart. Most of the advice in this industry is wrong anyway.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Including mine. I'm just better at being wrong in interesting ways.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: (A beat. She doesn't look offended. She looks pleased.)

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Nothing, technically. But now I know you're worth advising.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: I'm Amy Crawford. You've probably been warned about me.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Good. At least they're paying attention.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: Take the card.

lines.speaker: Amy Crawford
lines.text: (She extends a matte black card. No title. Just a number. She walks away without looking back.)
